opportunity_check.notes: Existing comments cover: task difficulty confound, lexical/semantic divergence, small sample, missing SC baseline, step-2 scope limit, model name error, anonymity violation. Table 5 internal contradiction NOT raised by anyone.

evidence_check.central_claim: Behavioral consistency strongly predicts correctness across all models
evidence_check.supporting_evidence_in_paper: Table 2: 32-55pp accuracy gap between low-variance and high-variance tasks across three models
evidence_check.contradicting_evidence_in_paper: Table 5: comparison questions show higher correctness (80.0%) than bridge questions (75.7%) but lower answer consistency (62.4% vs 76.6%)
evidence_check.paper_explanation_validity: Explanation ('explanations vary in comparison') does not explain why answer consistency (not explanation consistency) would be lower for constrained yes/no space

severe_issues.decision_impact: High - undermines claimed universality of consistency-correctness relationship; limits proposed runtime monitoring intervention

novelty_of_contribution: Non-redundant: no existing comment mentions Table 5 or the comparison vs bridge question type analysis
rationale: The Table 5 observation is (a) factual and directly verifiable, (b) not covered by any existing comment, (c) directly relevant to the accept/reject decision by undermining the central empirical claim's scope

key_strengths: Useful preliminary observation that variance correlates with failure on bridge-type retrieval tasks
key_weaknesses: Internal contradiction in Table 5 undermines central claim
Step-2 finding not generalized across models
